race.comment: GRACCONI (3) knuckled down strongly to get within a neck of the winner in second last start over 1400m at Echuca on debut. Can only be better for that experience, and the fitness from it, and strikes a winnable race at a more suitable distance. ADIRA RIVER (7) is out of the Caulfield Cup winner Jameka who finished fifth, beaten 4 lengths, when resuming from a spell last start over 1400m at Pakenham. Not a heap of depth here and should only be better suited at this trip. HIGH ALTITUDE (9) got back into the placings last start, which was the fourth run from a spell, so at peak fitness now and can go on with it. The two runs back for STORMING CAMELOT (6) have both been over 1300m, which is short of his best, so with them out of the way he can play a bigger role at 1600m. NIKEPHOROS (4) has had his fair share of chances and was well beaten last start, but is worth another chance going off the first-up effort when beaten two lengths over 1200m.
